name: netCDF4 (Python NetCDF File I/O)

auth.notes: Local I/O library; no auth required

performance.notes: C library backend (libnetcdf); fast for large multidimensional arrays

agent_readiness.notes: Python interface to netCDF-4/HDF5 files. from netCDF4 import Dataset. ds = Dataset('file.nc', 'r'). temp = ds.variables['temperature'][:]. ds.dimensions['time'].size. Write: ds = Dataset('out.nc', 'w'). ds.createDimension('time', None). var = ds.createVariable('temp', 'f4', ('time', 'lat', 'lon')). Lazy loading via slices. Compression and chunking. Used in climate science, oceanography, meteorology. xarray uses netCDF4 as backend for .nc files.

gotchas: Requires libnetcdf C library (conda install or system package)
CF conventions metadata can be complex
Prefer xarray over raw netCDF4 for labeled array operations
HDF5 files require h5py or netCDF4 (different APIs)
best_when: Climate, ocean, and meteorological data in .nc format
Reading/writing NetCDF4 files with dimension/variable metadata
When xarray's overhead is too heavy for simple reads
Scientific data exchange format for multidimensional arrays
avoid_when: Labeled operations (use xarray which wraps netCDF4)
HDF5 files without NetCDF conventions (use h5py)
summary: netCDF4 provides Python I/O for NetCDF4/HDF5 files used in climate science. Dataset('file.nc')['temperature'][:] reads arrays. createDimension/createVariable for writing. Lazy loading, chunking, compression. Standard format for climate, ocean, atmosphere data. xarray uses netCDF4 as backend — prefer xarray for labeled array operations. Requires libnetcdf C library.
